description_long: This is a visually striking karambit-style knife with a highly curved, single-edged blade. The blade and handle are decorated with alternating red, white, and green stripes, giving it a bold, almost candy-cane appearance. The handle features deep finger grooves for ergonomic grip and a prominent ring at the end, typical of karambit knives, allowing for secure handling and spinning tricks. The blade is relatively short but wide, with a pronounced curve and a pointed tip, designed for slashing motions. There is a small textured green area on the handle, likely for added grip. The entire object appears to be made of metal with a painted or coated finish, and no sheath or scabbard is present.
description: A vividly colored, curved karambit knife with red, white, and green stripes, ergonomic finger grooves, and a ringed handle.
description_view_0: The knife is seen edge-on, showing the thinness of the blade and handle, with the striped pattern visible along the edge.
description_view_1: The full side profile reveals the curved blade, finger grooves, ringed handle, and the colorful striped pattern.
description_view_2: A top-down view highlights the width of the handle and blade, with the ring and tip visible at either end.
description_view_3: The opposite side profile shows the same curved blade and handle features, with the striped pattern mirrored.
attribute_handle_design: contoured with deep finger grooves, ringed pommel, painted polymer or coated metal, textured grip area
attribute_ornamentation: striped red, white, and green pattern
